title: The Further Adventures: Droid World
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Droid World}} {{Audiobook |image=[[File:Droidworld.jpg]] |title=''The Further Adventures: Droid World'' |author=*[[Archie Goodwin]]
{{C|original comic}}{{SW|url=news/6-vintage-star-wars-picture-books|text=6 Vintage ''Star Wars'' Picture Books}} *[[Jymn Magon]] {{C|adaptation}} |narrator= |producer=[[Ted Kryczko]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.discogs.com/master/744782-No-Artist-Droid-World|text=Droid World on discogs.com|archivedate=20230416062603}} |director= |cover artist=[[Dick Foes]] |illustrator= |publisher=[[Buena Vista Records]] |release date=[[1983]] |rereleased= |media type=*[[Wikipedia:Phonograph record|7" 33 ⅓ rpm record]] {{C|453}} *[[Wikipedia:Cassette tape|Cassette tape]] {{C|153DC}} |run time=13 minutes''[[The Further Adventures: Droid World]]'' |discs=1 |cassettes=1 |pages=24{{SW|url=news/star-wars-in-the-uk-read-along-adventures|text=''Star Wars'' in the UK: Read-Along Adventures}} |isbn=none |timeline=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of the [[Star Wars (1977)]] issues [[Star Wars (1977) 52|52]], [[Star Wars (1977) 61|61]], and [[Star Wars (1977) 69|69]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]–3.5 ABY. The issues [[Star Wars (1977) 39|39]]–[[Star Wars (1977) 44|44]], which adapt [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] are also set in 3 ABY. Thus all events between can be placed in 3 ABY. Since [[The Further Adventures: Droid World]] adapts [[Star Wars (1977) 47|issue 47]] it must also take place in 3 ABY. |series=''[[Star Wars: The Further Adventures]]'' |preceded by= |followed by=[[The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs|''Planet of the Hoojibs'']] }} '''''The Further Adventures: Droid World''''' was a book-and-record in the ''[[Read-Along Adventures|Star Wars: The Further Adventures]]'' series. It was adapted from [[Star Wars (1977) 47|''Star Wars'' (1977) 47]] by [[Jymn Magon]] as part of the [[Buena Vista Records]]' [[Read-Along Adventures]] series. It was produced by [[Ted Kryczko]] and released in [[1983]] as book with [[Wikipedia:Phonograph record|7" 33 ⅓ rpm record]] and [[Wikipedia:Cassette tape|Cassette tape]] It is unknown who narrated the record as credits were not provided along with the book or record. ==Plot summary== When the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] captures a damaged new model of [[Imperial warbot]] on [[Xeron]] they decide to ask for assistance from the droid populated space station known as [[Kligson's Moon/Legends|Kligson's Moon]] in repairing the droid so the Alliance technicians can study it. [[Kligson/Legends|Kligson]] agrees to repair the droid, but [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] must accompany the warbot to observe the operation since Kligson intends to keep the droid after it's repaired. However, another droid on Kligson's moon has other ideas as the former Imperial droid [[Z-X3|Zee-Exthree]] plans to use parts of the warbot to repair his own war droid and lead a revolt against Kligson. However Kligson had anticipated this and sent a decoy to lure Zee-Exthree and his followers out into the open when he would launch his own warbot against them. With the battle over, R2-D2 and C-3PO record the schematics of the warbot and return to the Rebels, while Kligson moves his station from its current orbit to a more secret location where the Empire cannot find it. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from The Further Adventures: Droid World}} ===Editions=== *[[1983]]; Buena Vista Records; Vinyl LP record *1983; Buena Vista Records;
